meta.title: E21 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW 3 Series E21 engine — from the 1.6 M10 to the 2.3 M20 six. Real UK costs, honest reliability data. Compare prices from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 3 Series E21 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned E21 engine costs between £3,500 and £8,500 supply-only, depending on the engine — the M10 four-cylinder ranges £3,500–£7,200, while the M20 six-cylinder runs £4,500–£8,500. Fitting adds 10–18 hours of labour at £65–£90/hr. Use our calculator for a personalised quote.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 3 Series E21 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M10 2.0-litre fuel-injected 320i (125 bhp) is the most reliable E21 engine — it's the best-supported, most common, and delivers the smooth four-cylinder character that defined the early 3 Series. The carburettor models require more frequent maintenance, while the M20 six-cylinder adds timing belt complexity.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 3 Series E21?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain wear on M10 engines, timing belt failure on M20 engines (critical replacement every 60,000 miles or 5 years), carburettor or fuel injection system wear, and structural rust in floor pans, sills, and wheel arches. The Kugelfischer mechanical injection system on 320i models is reliable but requires specialist setup.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 3 Series E21 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: With proper maintenance, yes — these engines are robust and well-understood. The M10 is nearly indestructible if the timing chain tensioner is addressed. The M20 requires strict timing belt changes. Rust is the bigger enemy on these cars. For any E21, a thorough inspection and preventative maintenance are essential.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 3 Series E21 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: Early 316 (1.6-litre) carburettor models are underpowered for the car's weight and can be frustrating to drive. Also avoid any M20-engined car (320/6 or 323i) without documented timing belt replacement — a snapped belt destroys the engine. If you must have a six-cylinder, choose a 323i with full service history.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my E21 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain rattle on M10 engines, timing belt age or condition on M20 engines, persistent poor running or hesitation from carburettor/injection issues, blue smoke from the exhaust, or low compression on any cylinder are the key warning signs. Use our diagnostic tool for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 3 Series E21 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 70% BMW scrap threshold — but these are classic cars, so value assessment differs. Against a well-kept £15,000 E21, a £5,000 323i recon is 33% of the car's value. Against a tired £5,000 example, the same recon is 100% — we'd recommend walking away. For a cherished 323i or low-mileage example, the emotional value may justify the spend.

mainEntity.name: How long do BMW 3 Series E21 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: M10 four-cylinders can exceed 200,000 miles with proper servicing — the bottom end is exceptionally durable. M20 six-cylinders are similarly robust, provided the timing belt is changed every 60,000 miles or 5 years. Carburettor wear and injection system servicing are the main maintenance items.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the E21 and other 3 Series generations?

acceptedAnswer.text: The E21 is the first-generation 3 Series, produced from 1975 to 1983. It replaced the 02 Series and was exclusively a two-door coupé (with Baur convertibles available). It was succeeded by the E30 in 1982. The E21 is lighter, more basic, and more analogue than its successors, with no power steering on early models and a pure driving experience.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 3 Series E21 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is listed on the VIN plate, vehicle documentation, and on a casting on the engine block. For 1.6-litre fours, look for M10B16; for 1.8-litre fours, M10B18; for 2.0-litre fours, M10B20; for 2.0-litre sixes, M20B20; for 2.3-litre sixes, M20B23. Tell us your variant and year, and we'll confirm.

hero.h1: BMW 3 Series E21 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the original E21-generation BMW 3 Series — from the 1.6-litre M10 four-cylinder to the 2.3-litre M20 six-cylinder 323i. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and the carburettor-to-fuel-injection evolution that defined BMW's first 3 Series. Backed by real UK 3 Series enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: BMW 3 Series E21 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the E21-generation 3 Series specifically — from the 1975 launch through to 1983, covering the full range of M10 four-cylinder and M20 six-cylinder power units.

overview.intro: The BMW E21 is the first generation of the BMW 3 Series, a range of compact executive cars produced from June 1975 to December 1983. Designed by Paul Bracq, the E21 replaced the BMW 02 Series and was exclusively built as a two-door coupé (with Baur convertibles available from 1978–1981). At launch, all models used carburetted four-cylinder M10 engines of 1.6, 1.8, and 2.0 litres; fuel injection was introduced in late 1975 on the 320i. In 1977, a carburetted inline six-cylinder M20 engine (the 320/6) replaced both the 320 and 320i in many markets, and the range-topping 323i (fuel-injected 2.3-litre six) arrived in 1978. The E21 was superseded by the E30 in 1982.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: BMW E21 (first-generation 3 Series) • Predecessor: BMW 02 Series • Successor: BMW 3 Series E30 • Sibling body styles: Baur TC1 convertible (1978–1981)
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The E21-generation 3 Series is now a modern classic; enquiries are concentrated on M10 carburettor setup, M20 timing belt replacement, and rust remediation, with values ranging from £4,000 for entry-level 316 examples to £20,000+ for well-preserved 323is.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Six-Cylinder Entry — characterful but more complex

economicsRule.title: The E21 rule
economicsRule.text: A £5,000–£8,500 323i recon against a £6,000–£10,000 E21 sits at 50–140% of the car's value — on a sub-£7,000 example, that brushes our 70% BMW threshold, and we'll honestly tell you to walk away. Against a well-kept £15,000+ example, the same recon drops to a routine 33–57% — a repair decision, not a financial one. Value the car first.

keyTakeaways.question: Which E21 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: M10 316/318 — simplest carburettor setup, cheapest parts.

keyTakeaways.question: Which E21 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: M20B23 323i — injection system and timing belt service add cost.

keyTakeaways.question: Which E21 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: M10B20 320i — the best balance of performance, character, and running costs.

problems.title: M10/M20 Timing Chain/Belt Wear
problems.affectedModels: E21 all models — M10 four-cylinder (timing chain) and M20 six-cylinder (timing belt).
problems.rootCause: The M10's timing chain tensioner can wear over time, leading to chain rattle and potential timing issues. The M20 uses a rubber timing belt that requires replacement every 60,000 miles or 5 years — failure is catastrophic and bends valves.
problems.recommendation: On any E21, inspect the timing components immediately — a £400–£1,200 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than the £3,500–£8,500 replacement that follows if ignored.

cta.label: Compare M10/M20 engine prices for your E21 →

problems.title: M10 Carburettor/Injection System Wear
problems.rootCause: The Solex carburettors on early M10 engines suffer from wear in the throttle shafts and jets, leading to poor running, hesitation, and fuel consumption issues. The Kugelfischer mechanical injection system on 320i models is reliable but complex and requires specialist setup.
problems.recommendation: On any E21 with a carburettor, have the carburettor rebuilt at the first sign of hesitation — a £500–£1,200 rebuild is cheaper than the £3,500–£6,500 engine replacement that follows if ignored.

cta.label: Compare M10 engine prices for your E21 →

problems.title: E21 Body Rust (Structural)
problems.affectedModels: All E21 variants, particularly early cars and UK-market examples.
problems.rootCause: The E21, like many cars of its era, suffers from rust in the floor pans, sills, jacking points, and rear wheel arches. Structural rust can compromise the car's safety and its ability to pass an MOT.
problems.recommendation: On any E21, have a full rust inspection performed immediately — a £500–£2,000 preventative repair is cheaper than the £3,000–£8,000+ restoration that follows if ignored.

replacementCosts.h2: E21 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are E21 3 Series only. The 315 was introduced in 1981 as the entry-level model. The 320/6 (1977–1982) was the first E21 to use the M20 six-cylinder engine. The 323i (1978–1982) was the range-topping model. The Baur TC1 convertible was available from 1978 to 1981.
